explanations.7: The 'secrets' section here passes secret files into the Docker build process so the Dockerfile can access them securely without baking them into the image.
explanations.9: This references a secret named 'my_ssh_key' that's defined later in the file, making it available during the build.
explanations.12: This passes the filename 'id_ed25519' as a build argument so the Dockerfile knows which key file to use inside the build container.
explanations.15: This top-level 'secrets' section defines where Docker can find the actual secret files on your machine that should be made available to builds.
explanations.19: The ${HOME} syntax substitutes your home directory path at runtime, so Docker knows where to find your SSH key file.
